title: Invalid Numeric Input in Target Fields
intent: Verify Add Master Campaign: Invalid Numeric Input in Target Fields.
precondition: User is on the Master Campaign List Page. The "Add Master Campaign" button is visible. Target fields are implemented as numeric-only inputs (e.g., Total Views, Total GMV, Budget).

User focuses on a numeric target field, such as Total Views, Total GMV, or Budget.

User attempts to type non-numeric characters into the numeric field (e.g., letters "abc", symbols like "@#$", or other characters that are not allowed, except formatting like "," or "." if supported).

expected_result: Any non-numeric characters entered into numeric target fields are ignored and do not appear in the input field (except allowed numeric formatting such as "," or "." if supported by design).

The system therefore stores and processes only valid numeric values for target fields during Master Campaign creation.
No error message is required for typing non-numeric characters because the system prevents invalid characters from being entered in the first place.

title: Invalid Numeric Input in Target Fields
intent: Verify Add Master Campaign: Invalid Numeric Input in Target Fields.
precondition: User is on the Master Campaign List Page. The "Add Master Campaign" pop-up can be opened and contains numeric target fields (e.g., Total Views, Total GMV, Budget).
steps: User clicks "Add Master Campaign" to open the add pop-up.
The "Add Master Campaign" pop-up opens with empty fields.
User focuses on a numeric target field, such as Total Views, Total GMV, or Budget.
User attempts to type non-numeric characters into the field (e.g., letters "abc", symbols like "@#$"), excluding valid numeric formatting such as "," or "." if supported.
User observes the field behavior while entering these characters.
expected_result: The system rejects non-numeric characters in numeric fields: letters and unsupported symbols are ignored and do not appear in the input.
Only valid numeric characters (and optionally allowed separators like "," or ".") are accepted and visible in the target fields.
As a result, only valid numeric values are stored and used when creating the Master Campaign.
The user cannot accidentally submit alphabetic or invalid characters in any numeric target fields, reducing the risk of data quality issues.

title: Division by Zero for CPV/ROI
intent: Verify Master Campaign Detail Dashboard: Division by Zero for CPV/ROI.
precondition: The Master Campaign Detail Dashboard is open. The Campaign List contains at least one campaign where Total Views = 0 and/or Used Budget = 0.
steps: User navigates to the Campaign List section of the Master Campaign Detail Dashboard.
User reviews the campaign rows that have Total Views = 0 and/or Used Budget =
System attempts to calculate CPV (Used Budget / Total Views) and ROI (Total GMV / Used Budget) for each row.
expected_result: For campaigns where Total Views = 0, the CPV column displays N/A or - instead of Infinity, NaN, or a system error.
For campaigns where Used Budget = 0, the ROI column displays N/A or - instead of Infinity, NaN, or a system error.
All other campaigns with valid non-zero denominators show correctly calculated CPV and ROI values.
The UI remains stable, with no crashes or broken layouts caused by division by zero.

title: Data Load Error
intent: Verify Master Campaign Detail Dashboard: Data Load Error.

A backend error, timeout, or network failure occurs when the system requests data for Dashboard Overview, Pillar Contribution, and/or Campaign List.

No outdated or incorrect values are shown as "current" in those sections.
The overall page remains accessible (no crash), and the user may have an option to retry/refresh according to design.
Other unaffected sections (if any) can still display valid data, but nothing misleading is shown.

title: FYP Views Range Breakdown
intent: Verify Master Campaign Detail Dashboard: FYP Views Range Breakdown.
precondition: 1. The Master Campaign Detail Dashboard is open.
steps: The Master Campaign Detail Dashboard is open.
expected_result: The system displays the actual number of contents marked as FYP.
FYP is calculated as:
FYP = COUNT(content_link)
WHERE:
content.views ≥ 150% × median views of the Content Source account
AND:
content.views ≥ 10,000
The FYP calculation is based on the median views of the Content Source account, not the username account.
